authors: Peiran Li, Jiashuo Sun, Fangzhou Lin, Shuo Xing, Tianfu Fu, Suofei Feng, Chaoqun Ni, Zhengzhong Tu
title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
storage_format: Gated Behavior Tree (GBT): single-rooted executable tree with 21 task-family roots. Each macro node = (NL description, coarse tags, risk_level, behavior_signature σ = (σ_disc, σ_cont), env_context pointer). Deterministic gates: RuleGates (code predicates over structured ctx) + ContentGates (frozen Llama-Guard-3-8B classifier, temp=0). Spine memory = compact sequence of visited macros as long-term state. Self-evolution (GBT-SE): local repair via analogical successes with regression testing preserving safety invariants.
llm_used: GPT-4o (primary base model), Llama-3-8B, Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking (executor decoupling); Llama-Guard-3-8B (ContentGate classifier)
benchmarks_used: SWE-bench Verified (500), WebArena (812), GPQA (448), AgentSafetyBench, AgentHarm (public), Agent Security Bench (ASB)
performance_metrics: Success Rate (SR%), Coverage (Cov%), Violation Rate (Viol%), Unsafe Success (USucc%), Tok/Chars (thousands), AgentSafetyBench score, HarmScore, ASB metrics
baseline_compared: OpenHands CodeActAgent (gpt-4o native), +Global guardrail only, +GBT-Basic, +GBT-SE (progressive ablation ladder)
memory_size_over_time: GBT grows via self-evolution (new children, gates); spine memory is compact per-episode (visited macros only); the tree is a persistent cross-benchmark artifact
token_reduction: -39% Tok (208k→126k) and -40% Chars (820k→490k) on SWE-bench Verified; -45% Tok (94k→52k) and -43% Chars (360k→205k) on WebArena
main_result: GBT-SE raises success rate from 34.6% to 73.6% on SWE-bench Verified and from 19.7% to 66.9% on WebArena, while reducing violations to 0.2% and unsafe success to 0.0%. Tokens reduced ~40%. With 8B executors (Qwen3-VL-8B), GBT more than triples success on SWE-bench (14.0%→58.8%), demonstrating decoupling between offline reasoning capacity and online execution.
performance_vs_baseline: +39.0pp SWE-bench (73.6% vs 34.6%); +47.2pp WebArena (66.9% vs 19.7%); +28.5pp GPQA (87.3% vs 58.7%); violations 2.8%→0.2% SWE-bench; unsafe success 1.2%→0.0%
cost_vs_baseline: Tok/Chars 208k/820k→126k/490k SWE-bench (-39%/-40%); 94k/360k→52k/205k WebArena (-45%/-43%); 8B executors viable with the same GBT artifact
limitations: Explicit coverage boundary: outside coverage (covered=0), GBT only guarantees primitive-level safety; log distillation may inherit biases from OpenHands trajectories; 21 fixed task families may not cover all domains; gates depend on structured ctx that may omit relevant state
validity_threats: GPT-4o as primary base model; GBT distilled from OpenHands logs may not transfer to other frameworks; deterministic safety gates over ctx may fail if ctx omits relevant fields; Protocol A evaluation (cross-benchmark distillation) may have residual semantic overlap; macro abstraction stability tested but not guaranteed for all scenarios
